Your Healing Kitchen
Natural healing · Fourth trimester
Mineral tonic • Energy • Postpartum recovery

Adrenal Cocktail

Orange juice, coconut water & salt — replaces minerals depleted by stress and night feeds.
Makes 1 glass
Time 2 min
Caffeine none
For you · the 3pm slump
When to drink Mid-morning or the afternoon dip — between meals is ideal. Not a bedtime drink (vitamin C is mildly energising).

Why this heals you

  • Orange juice — vitamin C, which the adrenals use heavily verified
  • Coconut water — natural potassium verified
  • Sea salt — sodium to support fluid balance tradition
  • Whole-food minerals — gentler than a supplement tradition
Ingredients
½ cupFresh orange juice
½ cupCoconut water
⅛ tspSea salt
Method
1
Pour

½ cup orange juice + ½ cup coconut water into a glass.

2
Add salt

Stir in ⅛ tsp sea salt until it dissolves.

3
Sip

Drink slowly, ideally between meals. Over ice if you like.

Just for you, Mum

A well-known pick-me-up for the wired-but-tired days — whole-food minerals to steady the afternoon crash without caffeine.

Good to know
BestBetween meals, mid-afternoon.
Extra¼ tsp cream of tartar adds more potassium if you have it.
Why we say this
Vitamin C and potassium content are verified nutritional facts; the ‘adrenal support’ framing is popular wellness tradition, honestly labelled — not a medical claim.

Snacks
Drinks
Desserts
Food, not medical advice · non-diagnostic · made for this season of motherhood
